source: Arka metallurgy heat-treatment index (standard industrial practice; ASM Handbook-style procedures)

treatments.title: Heat Treatment of 6061 Aluminum (T6 Temper)
materials: Quench tank (water or polymer quenchant per spec)
safety: Wear heat-resistant gloves, face shield, and apron during quench.
Ensure adequate ventilation; molten quench media can spatter.
Follow furnace lockout/tagout before loading parts.
steps: Inspect parts for cracks, contamination, and correct alloy certification (6061).
Solution heat treat at 529 °C (985 °F) ±5 °C for 1 hour per inch of maximum cross-section (minimum 30 min).
Quench immediately in water (or specified polymer) to room temperature; agitate for uniform cooling.
Within 1 hour of quenching, artificial age at 160 °C (320 °F) for 18 hours (T6 temper).
Cool to room temperature in still air.
Verify hardness (~95 HB typical) or tensile properties per AMS/ASTM coupon tests.
Document furnace charts, lot numbers, and quench/age parameters for traceability.

treatments.title: Quench and Temper of 4140 Alloy Steel
materials: 4140 steel parts (normalized or annealed if heavily machined)
safety: Hot oil quench fire risk — keep Class B extinguisher nearby.
Parts can warp or crack if quench is too severe; use fixtures where needed.
Allow parts to cool below 66 °C before handling after temper.
steps: Preheat to 760 °C (1400 °F) to equalize temperature and reduce thermal shock.
Austenitize at 845-870 °C (1550-1600 °F) for 1 hour per inch of section.
Oil quench to below 65 °C; check for cracks and measure as-quenched hardness.
Temper immediately: 205 °C (400 °F) for ~48 HRC or 315 °C (600 °F) for higher toughness (~40 HRC).
Hold temper for 2 hours minimum; double temper for critical parts.
Air cool to room temperature after each temper cycle.
Finish grind or machine lightly; verify final hardness and dimensions.

treatments.title: Annealing of 304 Austenitic Stainless Steel
materials: Pickling/passivation chemicals if oxide scale present
safety: Pickling acids (HF/HNO₃ blends) are extremely hazardous — use fume hood and PPE.
Avoid carbon contamination from furnace fixtures on stainless.
steps: Clean parts to remove oils and iron contamination (iron-free abrasives only).
Anneal at 1040 °C (1900 °F), hold 30 min minimum for thin sheet; longer for heavy sections.
Rapid water quench or air cool depending on section thickness and distortion limits.
Remove heat-tint oxide by pickling or mechanical cleaning if corrosion resistance is critical.
Passivate per ASTM A967 if required for medical/food service.
Inspect for sensitization risk if slow cooled through 425-870 °C range.

treatments.title: Stress Relief of Brass Components
safety: Zinc fume exposure at high temperatures — ensure ventilation.
Do not exceed melting range; monitor furnace closely.
steps: Clean parts of cutting fluids and organic residues.
Stress relieve at 260-288 °C (500-550 °F) for 1-2 hours.
Slow cool in still air to room temperature.
Check dimensions on critical features; re-machine if springback occurred.
Optional: bright anneal at higher temperature only if full softening is required (product-specific).
